strategy: Two bf16 cuBLAS GEMMs (fp32 accumulate) plus a zero-copy (B,H,S,D) view of dgrad, with a side-stream wgrad co-issue gate

sources.content:
import torch

# SOL-ExecBench 004: attention output-projection backward.
# Two bf16 GEMMs sharing the same (already-materialized) inputs:
#   dgrad: grad_reshaped = grad_output_2d @ weight         (M x 2048, K=2048)
#   wgrad: grad_weight   = grad_output_2d^T @ reshaped_2d  (2048 x 2048, K=M)
#
# The reference up-casts to fp32 and calls fp32 GEMMs; because all operands are
# already bf16, bf16-in / fp32-accumulate / bf16-out reproduces the fp32
# reference within allowed tolerance. The cast + transpose+reshape tail is just
# a zero-copy (B,H,S,D) view of the dgrad output.
#
# Small-M headroom: for M <= SMALL_M the dgrad GEMM (M x 2048, K=2048) only
# lights ~ceil(M/128)*8 output tiles and underfills the 148 SMs. The wgrad
# GEMM (2048 x 2048 output) has 128 tiles and fills the machine. We therefore
# co-issue wgrad on a single side CUDA stream while dgrad runs on the caller's
# stream. For larger M both GEMMs saturate the device on their own, so we keep
# the sequential, single-stream path to avoid stream contention.

# Ensure bf16/fp16 matrix multiplies use fp32 reduction, matching the reference.
torch.backends.cuda.matmul.allow_bf16_reduced_precision_reduction = False
torch.backends.cuda.matmul.allow_fp16_reduced_precision_reduction = False

_NUM_HEADS = 32
_HEAD_DIM = 64
_HIDDEN = 2048
_SMALL_M = 1024
_STREAM_CACHE = {}


def _side_stream(device):
    idx = device.index
    s = _STREAM_CACHE.get(idx)
    if s is None:
        s = torch.cuda.Stream(device=device)
        _STREAM_CACHE[idx] = s
    return s


@torch.no_grad()
def run(
    grad_output: torch.Tensor,
    reshaped: torch.Tensor,
    weight: torch.Tensor,
):
    batch_size, seq_len, hidden_size = grad_output.shape
    M = batch_size * seq_len

    go2d = grad_output.reshape(M, hidden_size)
    rs2d = reshaped.reshape(M, hidden_size)

    if grad_output.is_cuda and M <= _SMALL_M:
        cur = torch.cuda.current_stream()
        side = _side_stream(grad_output.device)

        # The side stream must see the view-ops already queued on the caller's stream.
        side.wait_stream(cur)

        # Put the output-heavy wgrad on the side stream to fill idle SMs.
        with torch.cuda.stream(side):
            grad_weight = go2d.t().mm(rs2d)

        # Keep the underfilled dgrad on the caller's stream; both run concurrently.
        grad_reshaped_2d = go2d.mm(weight)

        # Rejoin before returning; make sure the caller's stream can safely
        # read grad_weight without the caching allocator recycling its storage.
        cur.wait_stream(side)
        grad_weight.record_stream(cur)
    else:
        grad_weight = go2d.t().mm(rs2d)
        grad_reshaped_2d = go2d.mm(weight)

    # Zero-copy reshape + transpose back to (B, H, S, D).
    grad_attn_output = grad_reshaped_2d.reshape(
        batch_size, seq_len, _NUM_HEADS, _HEAD_DIM
    ).transpose(1, 2)

    return grad_attn_output, grad_weight

import torch

# SOL-ExecBench 004: attention output-projection backward.
# Two bf16 GEMMs sharing the same (already-materialized) inputs:
#   dgrad: grad_reshaped = grad_output_2d @ weight         (M x 2048, K=2048)
#   wgrad: grad_weight   = grad_output_2d^T @ reshaped_2d  (2048 x 2048, K=M)
#
# The reference up-casts to fp32 and calls fp32 GEMMs; because all operands are
# already bf16, bf16-in / fp32-accumulate / bf16-out reproduces the fp32
# reference within allowed tolerance. The cast + transpose+reshape tail is just
# a zero-copy (B,H,S,D) view of the dgrad output.
#
# Small-M headroom: for M <= SMALL_M the dgrad GEMM (M x 2048, K=2048) only
# lights ~ceil(M/128)*8 output tiles and underfills the 148 SMs. The wgrad
# GEMM (2048 x 2048 output) has 128 tiles and fills the machine. We therefore
# co-issue wgrad on a single side CUDA stream while dgrad runs on the caller's
# stream. For larger M both GEMMs saturate the device on their own, so we keep
# the sequential, single-stream path to avoid stream contention.

# Ensure bf16/fp16 matrix multiplies use fp32 reduction, matching the reference.
torch.backends.cuda.matmul.allow_bf16_reduced_precision_reduction = False
torch.backends.cuda.matmul.allow_fp16_reduced_precision_reduction = False

_NUM_HEADS = 32
_HEAD_DIM = 64
_HIDDEN = 2048
_SMALL_M = 1024
_STREAM_CACHE = {}


def _side_stream(device):
    idx = device.index
    s = _STREAM_CACHE.get(idx)
    if s is None:
        s = torch.cuda.Stream(device=device)
        _STREAM_CACHE[idx] = s
    return s


@torch.no_grad()
def run(
    grad_output: torch.Tensor,
    reshaped: torch.Tensor,
    weight: torch.Tensor,
):
    batch_size, seq_len, hidden_size = grad_output.shape
    M = batch_size * seq_len

    go2d = grad_output.reshape(M, hidden_size)
    rs2d = reshaped.reshape(M, hidden_size)

    if grad_output.is_cuda and M <= _SMALL_M:
        cur = torch.cuda.current_stream()
        side = _side_stream(grad_output.device)

        # The side stream must see the view-ops already queued on the caller's stream.
        side.wait_stream(cur)

        # Put the output-heavy wgrad on the side stream to fill idle SMs.
        with torch.cuda.stream(side):
            grad_weight = go2d.t().mm(rs2d)

        # Keep the underfilled dgrad on the caller's stream; both run concurrently.
        grad_reshaped_2d = go2d.mm(weight)

        # Rejoin before returning; make sure the caller's stream can safely
        # read grad_weight without the caching allocator recycling its storage.
        cur.wait_stream(side)
        grad_weight.record_stream(cur)
    else:
        grad_weight = go2d.t().mm(rs2d)
        grad_reshaped_2d = go2d.mm(weight)

    # Zero-copy reshape + transpose back to (B, H, S, D).
    grad_attn_output = grad_reshaped_2d.reshape(
        batch_size, seq_len, _NUM_HEADS, _HEAD_DIM
    ).transpose(1, 2)

    return grad_attn_output, grad_weight
